```{r plast_MAP_2024}
  # need to change indexes if model is changed!
  if(mod.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[1] <= 0.05 | mod.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2] <= 0.05 | mod.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[4] <=0.05){
    
# list to save plots for each site
    plots.sub <- list()
    
    # dataframe to save outputs from posthoc tests
    df.posthoc <- as.data.frame(matrix(ncol = 3, nrow = length(sites)))
    rownames(df.posthoc) <- sites
    colnames(df.posthoc) <- c('map', 'slope', 'pval')
    df.posthoc$map <- sites.map
    
    for(s in 1:length(sites)){
      
      site <- sites[s]
      dat.sub <- subset(dat, MiniCG_Site_2024 == site)
      # skip if site is missing data
      if(sum(!is.na(dat.sub[,trait])) > 10){
        mod.sub <- lmer(paste0('GrowthIncrement_2024 ~', trait, '+ Pt + (1 | block) + (1 | Genotype)'),
                        data = dat.sub)
        # get pval for trait
        mod.sub.info <- Anova(mod.sub)
        df.posthoc[site, 'pval'] <- mod.sub.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[1]
        
        # get slopes from fixed effect of trait (effect on growth)
        df.posthoc[site, 'slope'] <- fixef(mod.sub)[2]
        
        # make title pretty
        maintitle <- paste(site, '\n', trait_names_df[trait, 'trait_names'], '\n', 'p = ', round(df.posthoc$pval[s], 3), sep = '')
        maintitle <- paste(strwrap(maintitle, width = 26), collapse = '\n')  
        
        # plot
        p <- ggplot(data = dat.sub, aes_string(y = 'GrowthIncrement_2024', x = trait, color = 'k2_tricho'), na.rm  = TRUE) +
          geom_point(na.rm = TRUE) +
          scale_color_gradient2(high = "darkolivegreen2", mid = "grey20", low = "dodgerblue2", midpoint = 0.5, name = 'P. trichocarpa\nancestry', guide = 'none') +
          ggtitle(maintitle) +
          geom_smooth(color = 'black', lty = ifelse(df.posthoc$pval[s] <= 0.05, 1,2), method = 'lm', formula = y ~ x, na.rm = TRUE) +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, size = 12)) +
          theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
        
        #plot(p)
        plots.sub[[s]] <- p
        
      }
    }

    # pvals are adjusted for summary plot
    df.posthoc$pvals.adj <- p.adjust(df.posthoc$pval, method = 'BH') 
    
    # plot panel with relationship for each garden
    # remove empty plots
    plots.sub <- plots.sub[lengths(plots.sub)>0]
    # plot in grid
    p1 <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.sub, ncol = 4)
    #plot(p1)
    
    # setup text showing significance
    sig_text <- paste0('Plasticity ', pval_stars(mod.info[1, 'Pr(>Chisq)']), '\n',
                       'Climate ', pval_stars(mod.info[2, 'Pr(>Chisq)']), '\n',
                       'Plasticity x Climate ', pval_stars(mod.info[4, 'Pr(>Chisq)']))
    
    # wrap title
    maintitle <- trait_names_df[trait, 'trait_names']
    maintitle <- paste(strwrap(maintitle, width = 26), collapse = '\n')
    
    # plot slope of fitness relationship vs climate
    p2 <- ggplot(df.posthoc, aes(x = map, y = slope), na.rm  = TRUE) +
      geom_smooth(method = 'lm', col = 'black', na.rm = TRUE) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
      geom_point(size = 5, stroke = 2, shape = ifelse(df.posthoc$pvals.adj <= 0.05, 16, 1), aes(col = map), na.rm = TRUE) +
      scale_color_gradient2(high = "steelblue2",  mid = 'grey', low = "sienna3", midpoint = mean(range(sites.map)), limits = range_map, name = 'Garden MAP (mm)') +
      ggtitle(maintitle,
              subtitle = sig_text) +
      xlab('Garden MAP') +
      ylab('Effect on Growth Increment') +
      geom_text_repel(label = rownames(df.posthoc), aes(x = map, y = slope), box.padding = 0.5, min.segment.length = 1, size = 4) +
        theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.position = 'none')
    plot(p2)
    
    plots.summ[[n]] <- p2
    names(plots.summ)[n] <- trait
    
     # add legend
    leg_plot <- ggplot(df.posthoc, aes(x = map, y = slope), na.rm  = TRUE) +
      geom_point(aes(color = map))+
      scale_color_gradient2(high = "steelblue2",  mid = 'grey', low = "sienna3", midpoint = mean(range(sites.map)), limits = range_map, name = 'Garden MAP (mm)') +
      theme(legend.position = 'right')
    
    legend <- cowplot::get_legend(leg_plot)
  }
}

plots.summ <- plots.summ[lengths(plots.summ)>0]

# add legend to beginning of plot list

plots.summ[[length(plots.summ)+1]] <- legend

p3 <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.summ, ncol = 3)
plot(p3)

#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxMAP_2024.png', p3, height = 6, width = 10)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxMAP_2024.pdf', p3, height = 6, width = 10)

# rename plots list for later use
plots.plast.map.24 <- plots.summ

```


### Garden TD

```{r plast_TD_2024}
################################################
# with TD

# reorder gardens by TD
tmp <- aggregate(dat$garden_TD_2024, by = list(dat$MiniCG_Site_2024), mean)
ord <- tmp$Group.1[order(tmp$x, decreasing = T)]
dat$MiniCG_Site_2024 <- factor(dat$MiniCG_Site_2024, levels = ord)

sites <- c("WI", "ID", "WYO", "EVERGREEN", "MSU", "OSU", "MORTON", "WSU", "PENN", "VA", "SU")

#sites <- levels(dat$MiniCG_Site_2024)
# remove UCM - height not measured
#sites <- sites[! sites == 'UCM']

sites.td <- dat$garden_TD_2024[match(sites, dat$MiniCG_Site)]

# to save results
mods.td <- list()

# list to save summary plot for each trait with significant effects on fitness
plots.summ <- list()


for(n in 1:length(traits)){
  
  trait <- traits[n]
  
  mod <- lmer(paste0('GrowthIncrement_2024 ~', trait,  '* garden_TD_2024 + Pt + (1 | MiniCG_Site/block) + (1 | Genotype)'),
              data = dat
  )
  
  print(car::Anova(mod))
  
  p1 <- plot_model(mod, vline.color = "black", show.values = T, type = 'std', 
                   title = paste('2024 Growth ~ ', trait, sep = ''))
  plot(p1)
  #plot_model(mod, type = 're')
  
  mods.td[[n]] <- mod
  names(mods.td)[n] <- trait
  
  #####################
  # posthoc tests
  
  # if either the climate or trait x climate are significant, run posthoc tests for each garden
  mod.info <- Anova(mod)
  # if any coefficients are significant, plot by garden
  # need to change indexes if model is changed!
  if(mod.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[1] <= 0.05 | mod.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2] <= 0.05 | mod.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[4] <=0.05){
    
    
# list to save plots for each site
    plots.sub <- list()
    
    # dataframe to save outputs from posthoc tests
    df.posthoc <- as.data.frame(matrix(ncol = 3, nrow = length(sites)))
    rownames(df.posthoc) <- sites
    colnames(df.posthoc) <- c('td', 'slope', 'pval')
    df.posthoc$td <- sites.td
    
    for(s in 1:length(sites)){
      
      site <- sites[s]
      dat.sub <- subset(dat, MiniCG_Site_2024 == site)
      # skip if site is missing data
      if(sum(!is.na(dat.sub[,trait])) > 10){
        mod.sub <- lmer(paste0('GrowthIncrement_2024 ~', trait, '+ Pt + (1 | block) + (1 | Genotype)'),
                        data = dat.sub)
        # get pval for trait
        mod.sub.info <- Anova(mod.sub)
        df.posthoc[site, 'pval'] <- mod.sub.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[1]
        
        # get slopes from fixed effect of trait (effect on growth)
        df.posthoc[site, 'slope'] <- fixef(mod.sub)[2]
        
        # make title pretty
        maintitle <- paste(site, '\n', trait_names_df[trait, 'trait_names'], '\n', 'p = ', round(df.posthoc$pval[s], 3), sep = '')
        maintitle <- paste(strwrap(maintitle, width = 26), collapse = '\n')  
        
        # plot
        p <- ggplot(data = dat.sub, aes_string(y = 'GrowthIncrement_2024', x = trait, color = 'k2_tricho'), na.rm  = TRUE) +
          geom_point(na.rm = TRUE) +
          scale_color_gradient2(high = "darkolivegreen2", mid = "grey20", low = "dodgerblue2", midpoint = 0.5, name = 'P. trichocarpa\nancestry', guide = 'none') +
          ggtitle(maintitle) +
          geom_smooth(color = 'black', lty = ifelse(df.posthoc$pval[s] <= 0.05, 1,2), method = 'lm', formula = y ~ x, na.rm = TRUE) +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, size = 12)) +
          theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
        
        #plot(p)
        plots.sub[[s]] <- p
        
      }
    }

    # pvals are adjusted for summary plot
    df.posthoc$pvals.adj <- p.adjust(df.posthoc$pval, method = 'BH') 
    
    # plot panel with relationship for each garden
    # remove empty plots
    plots.sub <- plots.sub[lengths(plots.sub)>0]
    # plot in grid
    p1 <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.sub, ncol = 4)
    #plot(p1)
    
    # setup text showing significance
    sig_text <- paste0('Plasticity ', pval_stars(mod.info[1, 'Pr(>Chisq)']), '\n',
                       'Climate ', pval_stars(mod.info[2, 'Pr(>Chisq)']), '\n',
                       'Plasticity x Climate ', pval_stars(mod.info[4, 'Pr(>Chisq)']))
    
    # wrap title
    maintitle <- trait_names_df[trait, 'trait_names']
    maintitle <- paste(strwrap(maintitle, width = 26), collapse = '\n')
    
    
    # plot slope of fitness relationship vs climate
    p2 <- ggplot(df.posthoc, aes(x = td, y = slope), na.rm  = TRUE) +
            geom_smooth(method = 'lm', col = 'black', na.rm = TRUE) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
      geom_point(size = 5, stroke = 2, shape = ifelse(df.posthoc$pvals.adj <= 0.05, 16, 1), aes(col = td), na.rm = TRUE) +
      scale_color_gradient2("orchid4",  mid = 'grey', low = "palegreen4", midpoint = mean(range(sites.td)), limits = range_td, name = 'Garden TD (°C)') +
      ggtitle(maintitle,
              subtitle = sig_text) +
      xlab('Garden TD') +
      ylab('Effect on Growth Increment') +
      geom_text_repel(label = rownames(df.posthoc), aes(x = td, y = slope), box.padding = 0.5, min.segment.length = 1, size = 4) +
        theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.position = 'none'
)
    
    plot(p2)
    
    plots.summ[[n]] <- p2
    names(plots.summ)[n] <- trait

    
     # add legend
    leg_plot <- ggplot(df.posthoc, aes(x = td, y = slope), na.rm  = TRUE) +
      geom_point(aes(color = td))+
      scale_color_gradient2("orchid4",  mid = 'grey', low = "palegreen4", midpoint = mean(range(sites.td)), limits = range_td, name = 'Garden TD (°C)') +
      theme(legend.position = 'right')
    
    legend <- cowplot::get_legend(leg_plot)
  }
}

plots.summ <- plots.summ[lengths(plots.summ)>0]

# add legend to beginning of plot list
plots.summ[[length(plots.summ)+1]] <- legend

p3 <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.summ, ncol = 3)
plot(p3)

#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxTD_2024.png', p3, height = 6, width = 10)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxTD_2024.pdf', p3, height = 6, width = 10)

# rename plots list for later use
plots.plast.td.24 <- plots.summ

```


## Plasticity plots for both years

```{r plasticity_plots_combine_years}

# merge lists of plots created above

plots.plast.mat <- c(plots.plast.mat.23, plots.plast.mat.24)
plots.plast.map <- c(plots.plast.map.23, plots.plast.map.24)
plots.plast.td <- c(plots.plast.td.23, plots.plast.td.24)


# MAT
pMAT <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.plast.mat, ncol = 4)
plot(pMAT)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxMAT.png', pMAT, height = 16, width = 14)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxMAT.pdf', pMAT, height = 16, width = 14)

# MAP
pMAP <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.plast.map, ncol = 3)
plot(pMAP)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxMAP.png', pMAP, height = 12, width = 14)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxMAP.pdf', pMAP, height = 12, width = 14)

# TD
pTD <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.plast.td, ncol = 3)
plot(pTD) 
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxTD.png', pTD, height = 12, width = 14)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityxTD.pdf', pTD, height = 12, width = 14)


```


```{r plot_panel}

# look at overlap in significant traits
g1 <- names(plots.trait.mat)
g2 <- names(plots.plast.mat)
g2 <- gsub('plasticity_rdpi_', '', g2 )
g2 <- gsub('_log', '', g2 )

overlap <- g1[g1 %in% g2]

overlap <- c("DOY_Stage6_2023", "licor_PhiPS2", "licor_Fs", 
"LMA_g_m2_2023", "DOY_last_budset_2024")

# subset of traits with significant interactions

panel <- grid.arrange(grobs = list(plots.trait.mat$leaf_thickness_avg_mm_2023,
                                   plots.trait.mat$DOY_Stage6_2023,
                                   plots.trait.mat$licor_PhiPS2, plots.trait.mat.24[[length(plots.trait.mat.24)]],
                                   plots.plast.mat$plasticity_rdpi_leaf_thickness_avg_mm_2023,
                                   plots.plast.mat.23$plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage6_2023 ,                                   plots.plast.mat$plasticity_rdpi_licor_PhiPS2), ncol = 4)

plot(panel)

#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffectsPanel_MAT_TraitAndPlasticity.png', panel, height = 8, width = 14)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffectsPanel_MAT_TraitAndPlasticity.pdf', panel, height = 8, width = 12)


grid.arrange(grobs = list(plots.trait.mat$LMA_g_m2_2023, plots.plast.mat$plasticity_rdpi_LMA_g_m2_2023), ncol= 2)

grid.arrange(grobs = list(plots.trait.mat$DOY_Stage6_2023, plots.plast.mat$plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage6_2023), ncol= 2)

##########################
# MAP
# look at overlap
g1 <- names(plots.trait.map)
g2 <- names(plots.plast.map)
g2 <- gsub('plasticity_rdpi_', '', g2 )
g2 <- gsub('_log', '', g2 )

overlap <- g1[g1 %in% g2]

dput(overlap)

#p <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.trait.map[overlap], ncol = 4)

panel <- grid.arrange(grobs = list(plots.trait.map$licor_gbw, plots.trait.map$leaf_area_cm2_2023, plots.trait.map$lower_stomata_density_mm2,             plots.plast.map$plasticity_rdpi_licor_gbw, plots.plast.map$plasticity_rdpi_leaf_area_cm2_2023_log, plots.plast.map$plasticity_rdpi_lower_stomata_density_mm2),
                      ncol = 3)
plot(panel)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffectsPanel_MAP_TraitAndPlasticity.png', panel, height = 8, width = 12)

#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffectsPanel_MAP_TraitAndPlasticity.pdf', panel, height = 8, width = 12)

```



# Is there a tradeoff between plasticity and traits?

If traits and their plasticity have a significant relationship, there could be a tradeoff between plasticity and expression of certain trait values

```{r tradeoff_plasticity_traits}

# list of traits

traits <- c("DOY_Stage2_2023", "DOY_Stage3_2023", "DOY_Stage6_2023", "DOY_Stage7_2023",  "DOY_last_budset_2023", "growing_season_days_2023", "licor_gsw", "licor_gbw", "licor_ETR", "licor_PhiPS2", "licor_Fs", "licor_Fm.", "leaf_thickness_avg_mm_2023", "LMA_g_m2_2023", "leaf_mass_g_2023", "leaf_area_cm2_2023", "lower_stomata_pore_length_mean_um", "upper_stomata_presence", "upper_stomata_pore_length_mean_um",  "upper_stomata_density_mm2", "lower_stomata_density_mm2", "stomata_ratio", "DOY_Stage2_2024", "DOY_Stage3_2024", "DOY_Stage6_2024", "DOY_Stage7_2024", "DOY_last_budset_2024", "growing_season_days_2024")

plasts <- paste('plasticity_rdpi_', traits, sep = '')
dput(plasts)

sum(! plasts %in% colnames(dat))
plasts[! plasts %in% colnames(dat)]

# manually edit names of plasticity columns that were log-transformed
dput(plasts)

plasts <- c("plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage2_2023", "plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage3_2023", 
"plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage6_2023", "plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage7_2023", 
 "plasticity_rdpi_DOY_last_budset_2023", 
"plasticity_rdpi_growing_season_days_2023", "plasticity_rdpi_licor_gsw", 
"plasticity_rdpi_licor_gbw", "plasticity_rdpi_licor_ETR", "plasticity_rdpi_licor_PhiPS2", 
"plasticity_rdpi_licor_Fs", "plasticity_rdpi_licor_Fm.", "plasticity_rdpi_leaf_thickness_avg_mm_2023", 
"plasticity_rdpi_LMA_g_m2_2023", "plasticity_rdpi_leaf_mass_g_2023_log", 
"plasticity_rdpi_leaf_area_cm2_2023_log", "plasticity_rdpi_lower_stomata_pore_length_mean_um", 
"plasticity_rdpi_upper_stomata_presence", "plasticity_rdpi_upper_stomata_pore_length_mean_um", 
"plasticity_rdpi_upper_stomata_density_mm2_log", "plasticity_rdpi_lower_stomata_density_mm2", 
"plasticity_rdpi_stomata_ratio_log", "plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage2_2024", 
"plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage3_2024", "plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage6_2024", 
"plasticity_rdpi_DOY_Stage7_2024",  
"plasticity_rdpi_DOY_last_budset_2024", "plasticity_rdpi_growing_season_days_2024"
)

cbind(traits, plasts)

################################################
# Do trait values predict trait plasticity, suggesting trade-offs?
# models
################################################
# reorder gardens by MAT
tmp <- aggregate(dat$garden_MAT_2023, by = list(dat$MiniCG_Site_2023), mean)
ord <- tmp$Group.1[order(tmp$x, decreasing = T)]
dat$MiniCG_Site_2023 <- factor(dat$MiniCG_Site_2023, levels = ord)

sites <- levels(dat$MiniCG_Site_2023)
# remove UCM - height not measured
sites <- sites[! sites == 'UCM']

sites.mat <- dat$garden_MAT_2023[match(sites, dat$MiniCG_Site)]


# to save results
mods.mat <- list()

# list to save summary plot for each trait with significant effects on fitness
plots.summ <- list()

for(n in 1:length(traits)){
  
  trait <- traits[n]
  plast <- plasts[n]
  
  print(paste('Model for:', trait, 'and', plast))
  
  # mode;
  mod <- lmer(paste0(trait, '~', plast,  ' + (1 | MiniCG_Site/block) + (1 | Genotype)'),
              data = dat
  )
  
  print(car::Anova(mod))
  
  p1 <- plot_model(mod, vline.color = "black", show.values = T, type = 'std', 
                   title = paste('2023 Growth ~ ', trait, sep = ''))
  plot(p1)
  #plot_model(mod, type = 're')
  
  mods.mat[[n]] <- mod
  names(mods.mat)[n] <- trait
  
  
  #####################
  # posthoc tests
  # if trait significantly predicts plasticity in full model, test in each garden
  mod.info <- Anova(mod)
  # if any coefficients are significant, plot by garden
  # need to change indexes if model is changed!
  if(mod.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[1] <= 0.05){
    
  # plot data for all sites
  p2 <- ggplot(data = dat, aes_string(y = plast, x = trait, color = 'k2_tricho'), na.rm  = TRUE) +
    geom_point(na.rm = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradient2(high = "darkolivegreen2", mid = "grey20", low = "dodgerblue2", midpoint = 0.5, name = 'P. trichocarpa\nancestry', guide = 'none') +
    geom_smooth(color = 'black', lty=1, method = 'lm', formula = y ~ x, na.rm = TRUE) +
    xlab(trait_names_df[trait, "trait_names"]) +
    ylab( paste('Plasticity (RDPI) of', trait_names_df[trait, "trait_names"])) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, size = 12)) #+
  #facet_wrap(~ MiniCG_Site_2023, drop = T, scales = 'free')
  
  plot(p2)
  
   p3 <- ggplot(data = dat, aes_string(y = plast, x = trait, color = 'k2_tricho'), na.rm  = TRUE) +
    geom_point(na.rm = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradient2(high = "darkolivegreen2", mid = "grey20", low = "dodgerblue2", midpoint = 0.5, name = 'P. trichocarpa\nancestry', guide = 'none') +
    geom_smooth(aes(group = MiniCG_Site_2023), lty=1, method = 'lm', formula = y ~ x, na.rm = TRUE) +
    xlab(trait_names_df[trait, "trait_names"]) +
    ylab( paste('Plasticity (RDPI) of', trait_names_df[trait, "trait_names"])) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, size = 12))
    
# list to save plots for each site
    plots.sub <- list()
    
    
    # dataframe to save outputs from posthoc tests
    df.posthoc <- as.data.frame(matrix(ncol = 4, nrow = length(sites)))
    rownames(df.posthoc) <- sites
    colnames(df.posthoc) <- c('mat', 'slope', 'cor', 'pval')
    df.posthoc$mat <- sites.mat
    
    for(s in 1:length(sites)){
      
      site <- sites[s]
      dat.sub <- subset(dat, MiniCG_Site_2023 == site)
      # skip if site is missing data
      if(sum(!is.na(dat.sub[,trait])) > 10){
        mod.sub <- lmer(paste0(trait, '~', plast,  ' + (1 | block) + (1 | Genotype)'),
              data = dat.sub
  )
        # get pval for trait
        mod.sub.info <- Anova(mod.sub)
        df.posthoc[site, 'pval'] <- mod.sub.info$`Pr(>Chisq)`[1]
        
        # get slopes from fixed effect of trait (effect of trait value on plasticity)
        df.posthoc[site, 'slope'] <- fixef(mod.sub)[2]
        
        # get standardized effect, equivalent to correlation
        eff.std <- effectsize::standardize_parameters(mod.sub)
        df.posthoc[site, 'cor'] <- eff.std[2, 'Std_Coefficient'] # 2nd row has coefficient for plasticity effect
        
        # plot
        p <- ggplot(data = dat.sub, aes_string(y = plast, x = trait, color = 'k2_tricho'), na.rm  = TRUE) +
          geom_point(na.rm = TRUE) +
          scale_color_gradient2(high = "darkolivegreen2", mid = "grey20", low = "dodgerblue2", midpoint = 0.5, name = 'P. trichocarpa\nancestry', guide = 'none') +
          ggtitle(paste(site, '\n', trait, '\n', 'p = ', round(df.posthoc$pval[s], 3), sep = '')) +
          geom_smooth(color = 'black', lty = ifelse(df.posthoc$pval[s] <= 0.05, 1,2), method = 'lm', formula = y ~x, na.rm = TRUE) +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1, size = 12)) +
          theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
        
        #plot(p)
        plots.sub[[s]] <- p
        
      }
    }

    # pvals are adjusted for summary plot
    df.posthoc$pvals.adj <- p.adjust(df.posthoc$pval, method = 'BH') 
    
    # plot panel with relationship for each garden
    # remove empty plots
    plots.sub <- plots.sub[lengths(plots.sub)>0]
    # plot in grid
    p1 <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots.sub, ncol = 4)
    #plot(p1)
    
    # setup text showing significance
    sig_text <- paste0('Trait ', pval_stars(mod.info[1, 'Pr(>Chisq)']))
    
    # wrap title
    maintitle <- trait_names_df[trait, 'trait_names']
    maintitle <- paste(strwrap(maintitle, width = 26), collapse = '\n')
    
    # plot slope of fitness relationship vs climate
    p2 <- ggplot(df.posthoc, aes(x = mat, y = cor), na.rm  = TRUE) +
      geom_point(size = 5, stroke = 2, shape = ifelse(df.posthoc$pvals.adj <= 0.05, 16, 1), aes(col = mat), na.rm = TRUE) +
      scale_color_gradient2(high = "red2", mid = "grey", low = "blue2", midpoint = mean(range(sites.mat)), limits = range_mat, name = 'Garden MAT (°C)') +
      geom_smooth(method = 'lm', col = 'black', na.rm = TRUE) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
      ggtitle(maintitle,
              subtitle = sig_text) +
      xlab('Garden MAT') +
      ylab('Correlation between Trait and Plasticity') +
      geom_text_repel(label = rownames(df.posthoc), aes(x = mat, y = cor), box.padding = 0.5, min.segment.length = 1, size = 4) +
        theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.position = 'none')
    plot(p2)
    
    plots.summ[[n]] <- p2
    names(plots.summ)[n] <- trait
    
    # add legend
    leg_plot <- ggplot(df.posthoc, aes(x = mat, y = slope), na.rm  = TRUE) +
      geom_point(aes(color = mat))+
      scale_color_gradient2(high = "red2", mid = "grey", low = "blue2", midpoint = mean(range(sites.mat)), limits = range_mat, name = 'Garden MAT (°C)') +
      theme(legend.position = 'right')
    
    legend <- cowplot::get_legend(leg_plot)
    
    
  }
}


plots.summ <- plots.summ[lengths(plots.summ)>0]

    
# add legend to beginning of plot list

plots2 <- plots.summ
plots2[[length(plots.summ)+1]] <- legend

p3 <- grid.arrange(grobs = plots2, ncol = 4)
plot(p3)

#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityTradeoffMAT2023.png', p3, height = 12, width = 14)
#ggsave(file = 'results/fitness/fitnessEffects_summaryPlot_traitPlasticityTradeoffMAT_2023.pdf', p3, height = 12, width = 14)

# rename plots list for later use
plots.plast.mat.23 <- plots.summ


```
